scope_note: QA Witt Tower International A-Coordinate Generalization Certificate [462]. Tests whether the QA A2-derived coordinate a=b+2*e (raw, not mod-reduced) on daily return rank-bins in Z/27Z predicts next-day price direction outside the US training set. INTERNATIONAL ETFs: EWJ (Japan), EWG (Germany), EWL (Switzerland), EWU (UK), EWA (Australia), EWC (Canada). MAPPING: daily log-return -> rank in N-return window -> bin=floor(rank*27/N) in Z/27Z; consecutive pairs (b=bins[t-1], e=bins[t]) -> A2 coord a=b+2*e (raw, not mod-reduced). PREDICTION GROUP: a<=6. PRIMARY RESULT: INTL pooled n=1099, mean=+0.42%, pos=55.5%, perm_p=0.0000. PER-ETF: EWJ(p=0.0004), EWG(p=0.0048), EWL(p=0.0002), EWU(p=0.0000), EWA(p=0.0000) significant (5/6); EWC null (p=0.126, highly US-correlated market). US BASELINE (cert [461]): n=936, mean=+0.37%, perm_p=0.0002. CERTIFIED: (C1) INTL pooled perm_p<0.001 PASS. (C2) 5/6 individually significant PASS. (C3) INTL mean=+0.42%>=0.30% PASS. (C4) INTL pos=55.5%>52% PASS. (C5) EWC mean=+0.18%>0 (direction preserved) PASS. (C6) effect magnitude comparable to US PASS. Structural parents: cert [110] (Witt Tower framework), cert [461] (daily a<=6 US). Primary source: Fama EF (1970) doi:10.2307/2325486. Validated 2026-06-19.
